summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Instant pay can boost low-income workers’ savings habits: report
articles.description: Despite consumer advocates’ concerns about earned wage access fees, researchers found modest usage can help with financial planning.

articles.title: Social media blasted over fraud
articles.description: The Consumer Federation of America estimates the annual cost of online scams at $119 billion, pointing to social media as a big part of the problem.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard face U.K. challenge
articles.description: “In seeking to reduce its reliance on Visa and Mastercard, the United Kingdom banking sector is justified, given the perceived risk that the two main schemes have too much control over the market,” a European executive says.

articles.title: Fraud battle calls for cross-sector effort
articles.description: As criminals adopt AI-enabled tactics, financial institutions need to consider data-sharing with other industries, fraud-fighting professionals contend.

articles.title: Engine aims to fix SMB cards
articles.description: Engine, a business-travel startup, says its smaller-business customers aren’t served by current travel and entertainment card players in the market.

articles.title: Global Payments feels Middle East impact
articles.description: The payments services provider said the conflict in the Middle East is taking a toll as some of its airline customers are impacted.

articles.title: Cash-strapped homeowners tap BNPL
articles.description: Researchers at the JPMorgan Chase Institute found that homeowners are most likely to use buy now, pay later tools when their bank accounts are low or their credit cards are maxed out.

articles.title: PayPal pivots for 2026
articles.description: The digital payment player aims to keep scaling its collection of integrated businesses, the company’s chief financial and operating officer said.
